Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of Buddy Clean.

id.description: Unique identifier for the customer. This is the Firebase Auth UID.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed by a customer.

serviceIds.description: References to Services. (Relationship: Order N:N Service)

orderDate.description: The date the order was placed.

serviceDate.description: The date the service is scheduled for.

totalAmount.description: The total amount for the order.

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., Razorpay, Cash on Delivery).

status.description: The status of the order (e.g., Pending, Confirmed, Completed, Cancelled).

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a cleaning service offered by Buddy Clean.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., Home Cleaning, Car Wash).

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: Represents a contact message submitted by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the contact message.

name.description: The name of the person submitting the message.

email.description: The email address of the person submitting the message.

createdAt.description: The date and time the message was created.

AdminRole.title: AdminRole
AdminRole.description: Represents an administrator role within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the administrator role. This should match the Firebase Auth UID.

isAdmin.description: Indicates whether the user has administrator privileges.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to users.

recipients.description: Array of customer UIDs who will receive the notification.

createdAt.description: The date and time the notification was created.

sentAt.description: The date and time the notification is scheduled to be sent.

definition.description: Stores customer profile information. The customerId is the Firebase Auth UID, providing implicit ownership.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the customer.

definition.description: Stores orders for each customer. Includes denormalized 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the customer.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact message.

definition.description: Identifies administrators by their Firebase Auth UID. Existence of the document grants admin privileges.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the administrator.

definition.description: Stores notifications sent to users. Publicly readable.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For example, the `orders` subcollection includes the `customerId` which enables validation of ownership without needing to `get()` the parent `customer` document. This allows atomic operations and simplifies security rules. Structural Segregation is implemented by separating user data (`/customers/{customerId}`) from global data (`/services/{serviceId}`, `/contacts/{contactId}`, `/roles_admin/{uid}`). Access Modeling follows consistent patterns: Path-Based ownership is used for private data (`/customers/{customerId}/orders/{orderId}`), and Existence over Content for global roles (`/roles_admin/{uid}`). The structure supports QAPs by allowing secure list operations based on ownership, without filtering based on document data.

*   `/customers/{customerId}`: Stores customer profiles. `customerId` matches the Firebase Auth UID, providing implicit ownership.
*   `/customers/{customerId}/orders/{orderId}`: Stores orders for each customer. The `customerId` is denormalized, enabling secure rules without `get()` calls. This supports the Customer 1:N Order relationship. This structure also enables listing orders only for the logged-in user.
*   `/services/{serviceId}`: Stores details of cleaning services. Accessible to all users for listing, but create/update/delete operations are restricted to admins.
*   `/contacts/{contactId}`: Stores contact messages. Accessible to all users for creating new contact messages, but listing/reading/deleting are restricted to admins.
*   `/roles_admin/{uid}`: Indicates administrator roles. Uses Existence over Content (DBAC) to grant admin privileges, simplifying security rules. If a document exists, the user is an admin.
* `/notifications/{notificationId}`: Stores notifications for users. This collection is publicly readable so that all users (authenticated or not) can see announcements, but only admins can create, update, or delete them.
